phase3_findings.description: docs/DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md described devflow.yml's flip step as keyed on steps.claude.outcome failure|cancelled while the shipped guard carries a third disjunct (steps.engine.outputs.is_error == 'true') that the same bullet's opening sentence enumerates. A diff-added doc line false against HEAD.
flip-review-progress-failed.sh discarded workpad.py's stderr via 2>/dev/null, so the read-failure breadcrumb named the arm but not the underlying gh cause (403 token scope vs rate limit vs 5xx).
Empty-PR and empty-marker converged on one shared usage breadcrumb; the empty-PR path is reachable from finalize_check's pr_number output.

The two 'wires the helper' pins grep only the filename, which also appears on the FLIP_HELPER= path-assignment lines — so deleting the actual `bash "$FLIP_HELPER"` invocation in either workflow left every pin GREEN, gutting the flip undetected.

The helper's NOSTATUS arm is untested and the '**Status:** 🚀 Reviewing' seed-shape coupling with skills/review/SKILL.md is unpinned; a seed drift would route every flip to the NOSTATUS no-op while all pins stayed green.
The implement-side flip_to_failed guard has no behavioral coverage (YAML-embedded shell cannot execute in a bash suite); only static pins cover it.
Changeset prose 'on every fail-loud exit' reads over-broad in isolation; the schema states the precise interim-guarded form.
_progress_phase_for_status docstring's unmapped-status example named only (Blocked); Failed is now a second unmapped terminal status.

bash's ${c: -300} yields the EMPTY string when the string is shorter than 300 (zsh clamps, bash does not), so the Cause: breadcrumb was blank for every realistic short gh error — the exact messages it exists to surface. The tests asserted only the arm name, never the cause content.

fix_decisions.claim_text: devflow.yml flip step misses the engine is_error arm
fix_decisions.evidence: Calibrated UP: engine_is_error is parsed AFTER the claude step (devflow-runner.yml:600-632), so a step can exit success with is_error=true; the arm is reachable and the issue's Desired Behavior names it. Fail-open coverage hole in a guard this diff introduced -> park-calibration shape 1 forbids parking.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the implement-side flip_to_failed guard is verified only by static pins, never executed
fix_decisions.evidence: Accepted residual: YAML-embedded shell cannot execute in the bash suite. Mitigated — the static pins quote the guard's exact text (CLASS=interim, FAIL=1), so an inversion or deletion goes RED; and the resume-arm absence pin was hardened in iteration 3 to be indentation-agnostic with a positive control. Extracting the guard into a testable helper is a separate refactor.
